title: system test: Add --log-driver test for podman run
body: Add a test for testing different log driver in podman run

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *ypu* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Thanks @rhatdan  for your comments. Update the code but found some bugs in current version. The test will failed:
1. podman logs can not read from journald and w
2. --log-drover json-file will not store the logs to a file

Report an issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7476 for record the problems.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
This is (mostly) resolved in #7758, although that one does not test `--log-opt` nor journald.

Hi @rhatdan Looks like @edsantiago already write another test for cover the --log-driver and the issue I report still not fixed yet. I'd like close this one now. And will add some check steps based on Ed's test after the issue is fixed.

Thanks @rhatdan and @edsantiago for all your review and comments.
